translated: I’m sorry, but I’m unable to translate this Ntcham text.

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Kutaa wúntì ŋwìiń nyáń ní Jaakɔb yíkì Peeniyeel. U-kpìiŋŋu puee, u nìn dúlifí ní.
translated: When the night fell Jacob named Peniel. He prayed, and he was humbled.

ref: One day, Abraham told Abimelech, “Some of your servants have taken over one of my wells.”
text: Ama ní Abraam dū lɔkɔ un u gbìn̄ ní Abimeeleeki tɔntɔnliibi ŋá mpɔn ki fōōyee bɔti ki bíl̀ u-nimbiin.
translated: When Abraham was old and his son Isaac was born, he went to Abimelech’s well to drink water.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Jaakɔb nìn néeń Raseel, ní ki bí u-wee yii: «M làá tɔ̄ ditundi kí tīī-si abin aluli á dū a-bisal uwaal Raseel kí tīī-mi.»
translated: Jacob and his son Raseel, when he said: “I will not go until I have taken care of the people of Raseel; I will stay.”

ref: He put the personal servant back in his old job
text: Ní Faaroo gíiǹ bin bàtī ndaam ki tìì-uee kpiliu tundi ki ŋúbiǹ-u, ní úǹ bātì ndaam ki tīī-u.
translated: The father who works hard and teaches his children, they also help him.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: If you go to Unimboti and stay there for a while, you will find Unimboti again. You will stay there for a long time.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: If you want to read a book, you must first open it, and if you open it, you will find the knowledge inside.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham went to the river, but he did not find water, and he returned home feeling very sad.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: When we gather together in the house, Jacob also comes to the meeting and we all speak about the future, and we will continue to work together.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob is my father, and he is very strong. He lives in Unimboti, near the river.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: If you are going to Unimboti, you must first go to Unimboti. Then you will find the place you are looking for.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: If you want to read a book, you must do so; if you want to read a book, you must do so; and if you want to write a book, you must do so, for that is the way to learn.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: When we gathered together in the house, Jacob also came and brought food for us to eat, and we all shared it together.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: My name is Jacob. I am from Unimboti, a place near the river.
